{"step_by_step_thinking": "SABE prophylaxis is typically required for patients undergoing certain cardiac surgeries or procedures to prevent bacterial endocarditis. MR (mitral regurgitation) and ASD (atrial septal defect) are conditions that may require prophylaxis before dental procedures, but CABG (coronary artery bypass graft) surgery generally does not require SABE prophylaxis unless the patient has specific risk factors. MS (multiple sclerosis) is not a cardiac condition and does not typically require SABE prophylaxis. Therefore, the condition that does not require SABE prophylaxis is MS.", "answer_choice": "C"}
